 {"id":"git_safety_guard-perf","title":"Implement performance benchmarks for heredoc detection","description":"# Performance Benchmark Suite

## Objective

Create benchmarks to measure and enforce performance budgets for heredoc detection.

## Performance Budgets

| Operation | Budget | Panic Threshold |
|-----------|--------|----------------|
| Tier 1 regex check | < 10μs | > 100μs |
| Heredoc extraction | < 500μs | > 2ms |
| Language detection | < 50μs | > 200μs |
| AST parsing (small) | < 2ms | > 10ms |
| AST parsing (large) | < 10ms | > 50ms |
| Full pipeline | < 15ms | > 50ms |

## Benchmark Implementation

Using criterion for statistical benchmarks:

```rust
// benches/heredoc_perf.rs
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion, BenchmarkId};

fn bench_tier1_regex(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let cases = [
        ("simple", "git status"),
        ("heredoc_marker", "python3 << 'EOF'\nprint('hi')\nEOF"),
        ("inline_script", "bash -c 'echo hello'"),
        ("long_command", &"x".repeat(10000)),
    ];

    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("tier1_regex");
    for (name, cmd) in cases {
        group.bench_with_input(
            BenchmarkId::new("might_contain_heredoc", name),
            cmd,
            |b, cmd| b.iter(|| might_contain_heredoc(cmd)),
        );
    }
    group.finish();
}

fn bench_full_pipeline(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark complete heredoc check flow
}

criterion_group!(benches, bench_tier1_regex, bench_full_pipeline);
criterion_main!(benches);
```

## Cargo.toml Addition

```toml
[[bench]]
name = "heredoc_perf"
harness = false

[dev-dependencies]
criterion = "0.5"
```

## CI Integration

Add to CI pipeline:

```yaml
- name: Run benchmarks
  run: cargo bench --bench heredoc_perf -- --noplot
```

## Regression Detection

Store baseline and compare:

```bash
# Save baseline
cargo bench --bench heredoc_perf -- --save-baseline main

# Compare against baseline
cargo bench --bench heredoc_perf -- --baseline main
```

## Test Data

Create fixtures:

- `benches/fixtures/small_heredoc.txt` (10 lines)
- `benches/fixtures/medium_heredoc.txt` (100 lines)
- `benches/fixtures/large_heredoc.txt` (1000 lines)
- `benches/fixtures/real_world_samples/` (actual heredocs from the wild)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Benchmarks exist for Tier 1 trigger, extraction, and at least one AST match path.
- A baseline is established and CI can detect meaningful regressions (thresholds documented).
- Benchmarks are deterministic enough to be useful (noise handled via criterion baselines).
- Fixture sizes cover small/medium/large and include at least one “worst-case” style input.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-phpp","title":"Define PHP destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"# PHP Destructive Patterns for Heredoc Scanning

## Objective

Define PHP heredoc matchers focused on **irreversible operations** (filesystem deletion and literal destructive shell payloads), while minimizing false positives.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on destructive outcomes.
- Do not include broad “dynamic code execution” patterns (`eval`, dynamic include) as default denies; too common and not necessarily destructive.

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Shell execution with destructive payload (high signal)

- `system("rm -rf ...")` / `` `rm -rf ...` `` (literal)
- `exec("git reset --hard ...")` (literal)

### 2) Recursive / high-risk filesystem operations

- custom recursive delete helpers are hard to statically detect; start with high-signal built-ins:
  - `unlink($path)` / `rmdir($path)`

Severity guidance:

- `Error` only when `$path` is a clearly catastrophic literal target.
- Otherwise `Warning`.

### 3) Obvious destructive SQL literals (optional)

If we can match literal `DROP` / `TRUNCATE` strings passed to `query/exec`, tag as `Warning` by default.

## False positive mitigation

- Do not flag `exec($VAR)` as deny.
- Require negative fixtures for:
  - harmless use of `exec` (e.g., `exec("php -v")`)
  - code printing dangerous strings

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines a PHP pattern set aligned with severity taxonomy and focused on destructive outcomes.
- Includes at least 5 positive and 5 negative fixtures.
- No default hard denies unless the matched operation is clearly catastrophic.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","title":"Implement robustness and edge case handling for heredoc parsing","description":"# Robustness and Edge Case Handling for Heredoc/Inline Script Analysis

## Objective

Ensure heredoc detection + extraction + AST matching are *robust by construction*:

- never panic
- never hang
- never allocate unbounded memory
- fail-open on malformed input/timeouts (hook safety)

This task focuses on **edge cases** and **hardening** (the stuff that only shows up in the wild).

## Background

Heredoc and inline-script analysis is the highest-risk path for stability because inputs are:

- multiline
- potentially huge
- often malformed (especially from agents)
- can include confusing quoting/indentation variants

A single crash/hang in this path makes users disable dcg.

## Hardening requirements (v1)

### 1) Size limits (must be enforced)

- enforce a max extracted body size and max line count
- limits must be configurable (see global limit work) and *never* exceed sane defaults

Behavior:

- if limits are exceeded: **skip deep analysis** and fail-open
- record a structured log/event when this happens (so it is debuggable)

### 2) Deadline / timeout protection (must be enforced)

- tier 2 extraction must be bounded
- tier 3 AST parsing/matching must be bounded
- if using external processes (ast-grep), they must have a hard timeout + kill

Behavior:

- on timeout: skip deep analysis, fail-open, log `skipped_due_to_budget`

### 3) Malformed heredoc handling (must be graceful)

Handle without panic:

- missing delimiter
- unterminated heredoc
- invalid delimiter tokens
- invalid indentation stripping cases (`<<-`)
- multiple heredocs in a single command

### 4) Non-UTF8 / binary-ish content (must be safe)

- detect binary-like payloads (null bytes, high non-printable ratio)
- do not feed binary-ish input into AST parsing

Behavior:

- skip analysis and fail-open; log `skipped_binary_content`

### 5) Multiple heredocs / nested triggers (bounded)

- support multiple heredocs per command up to a small cap
- if nested heredoc triggers appear inside a body:
  - treat as **low-confidence** signal initially (WARN/log), not auto-DENY
  - avoid recursion in v1; recursion can be a separate follow-up if needed

## Observability requirements

When deep analysis is skipped, the system must surface why (in trace/log):

- exceeded size limits
- deadline/budget exceeded
- parse error / malformed input
- binary-ish content detected

## Acceptance criteria

- No panics for arbitrary inputs (validated via fuzz/proptest targets).
- Extraction and analysis never exceed configured limits/budgets.
- Skips are visible in `dcg explain` and structured logs.
- Multiple heredocs are handled up to the configured cap.

## Tests

- Unit tests:
  - oversized heredoc → skip + fail-open + trace/log marker
  - unterminated heredoc → skip + fail-open
  - binary-ish heredoc → skip + fail-open
  - multiple heredocs parsed up to cap

- Fuzzing:
  - heredoc extraction fuzz target must find no panics

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-99e.10` global size limits (single source of truth)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.14` evaluation time budget/deadline semantics
- `git_safety_guard-y4j` extraction implementation
- `git_safety_guard-tlog` logging/trace integration for heredoc path
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMill","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T02:00:11.048164731-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T02:00:11.048164731-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: size limits, binary detection, malformed input handling, multiple heredoc bounds, SkipReason observability. All 257 tests pass. Timeout enforcement deferred until Tier 3 AST matching. Fuzz targets tracked separately.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2ta","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:46:02.228572246-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-y4j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:47:10.592550683-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:34.385023947-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan","title":"Epic: Repo scanning + integrations (pre-commit/CI)","description":"# Epic: Repo scanning + integrations (pre-commit/CI)

## Why this epic exists

The hook protects *interactive* command execution, but teams also need protection against destructive commands that get **committed into repositories**:

- CI workflows (`run:` steps) that include dangerous commands
- Dockerfiles (`RUN`) that include irreversible operations
- shell scripts and Makefiles that do destructive cleanup
- Terraform provisioners that run destructive shell

Catching these in review (pre-commit/CI) prevents incidents and makes `dcg` feel like a serious, team-scale safety layer.

Crucially, this must be done **without creating new false positives** that annoy users at org scale.

## Goals

1. Implement `dcg scan` that extracts **executable command contexts** from files and evaluates them using the **same shared evaluator** as hook/CLI.
2. Provide pre-commit and CI workflows that are "warn-first" and configurable (`--fail-on error|warning|none`).
3. Produce high-quality, actionable output:
   - stable rule IDs (`pack_id:pattern_name`)
   - file:line[:col] locations
   - short reason + optional suggestion
4. Keep performance bounded:
   - scan only changed files by default in CI
   - cap file size and number of findings

## Non-goals (initially)

- Building a full static-analysis engine for arbitrary languages.
- Grepping entire repos by default.

## Safety constraints

- Scanners must be extractor-based, not naive substring grep.
- Scanner output must make it obvious what is **executed** vs **documentation**.
- Default behavior should be conservative (warn-first rollout), with hard-fail only for high-confidence catastrophic rules.

## Success Criteria

- `dcg scan --staged` works locally and reports actionable findings.
- A minimal GitHub Actions workflow can run `dcg scan` on PR diffs and surface results.
- Unit tests cover extractor correctness for all MVP file types.

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan","title":"Epic: Repo scanning + integrations (pre-commit/CI)","description":"# Epic: Repo scanning + integrations (pre-commit/CI)

## Why this epic exists

The hook protects *interactive* command execution, but teams also need protection against destructive commands that get **committed into repositories**:

- CI workflows (`run:` steps) that include dangerous commands
- Dockerfiles (`RUN`) that include irreversible operations
- shell scripts and Makefiles that do destructive cleanup
- Terraform provisioners that run destructive shell

Catching these in review (pre-commit/CI) prevents incidents and makes `dcg` feel like a serious, team-scale safety layer.

Crucially, this must be done **without creating new false positives** that annoy users at org scale.

## Goals

1. Implement `dcg scan` that extracts **executable command contexts** from files and evaluates them using the **same shared evaluator** as hook/CLI.
2. Provide pre-commit and CI workflows that are "warn-first" and configurable (`--fail-on error|warning|none`).
3. Produce high-quality, actionable output:
   - stable rule IDs (`pack_id:pattern_name`)
   - file:line[:col] locations
   - short reason + optional suggestion
4. Keep performance bounded:
   - scan only changed files by default in CI
   - cap file size and number of findings

## Non-goals (initially)

- Building a full static-analysis engine for arbitrary languages.
- Grepping entire repos by default.

## Safety constraints

- Scanners must be extractor-based, not naive substring grep.
- Scanner output must make it obvious what is **executed** vs **documentation**.
- Default behavior should be conservative (warn-first rollout), with hard-fail only for high-confidence catastrophic rules.

## Success Criteria

- `dcg scan --staged` works locally and reports actionable findings.
- A minimal GitHub Actions workflow can run `dcg scan` on PR diffs and surface results.
- Unit tests cover extractor correctness for all MVP file types.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.1","title":"Design: dcg scan extraction model + output schema","description":"## Objective

Define the extraction model, CLI surface area, and output schema for `dcg scan` so implementation is coherent and testable.

## Background

Repo scanning is where false positives can explode:

- YAML contains lots of strings; only some are executed.
- Dockerfiles have multiple instruction forms; only some invoke a shell.
- Makefiles have recipes, variables, and docs.

If we design output + extraction boundaries up front, we can be conservative and predictable.

## Proposed `dcg scan` UX (v1)

### Commands

- `dcg scan --staged`
- `dcg scan --paths <path>...`
- `dcg scan --git-diff <rev-range>` (CI/PR default)

### Output

- `--format pretty|json`
- Findings include:
  - `file`, `line`, optional `col`
  - `extractor_id` (e.g., `github-actions.run`, `dockerfile.run`)
  - `extracted_command` (possibly truncated/redacted)
  - `decision` (allow|warn|deny)
  - `severity` (info|warning|error)
  - `rule_id` (`pack_id:pattern_name`) when matched
  - `reason`
  - optional `suggestion`

### Policy knobs

- `--fail-on error|warning|none`
- `--max-file-size` (default 1MB)
- `--max-findings` (default 50)
- `--exclude` glob patterns
- `--redact` / truncation controls

## Extraction contract

Each extractor returns a list of `ExtractedCommand`:

- `{ file, line, col?, kind, command, metadata }`
- `kind` identifies an *execution context* (not a doc string).

This is intentionally the same philosophy as the execution-context classifier for command lines:

- Be explicit about what is executed.
- Everything else is data.

## MVP file types (must be covered)

- `.sh` (shell script)
- `Dockerfile` (`RUN` shell form)
- GitHub Actions workflow YAML (`steps[].run`)
- `Makefile` recipe lines

## Acceptance criteria

- Output schema is stable enough to write golden tests.
- Extractor boundaries are conservative and clearly documented.
- Design maps cleanly to a small set of Rust structs/enums.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Determinism
- Extraction output and scan JSON output must be deterministic:
  - stable file ordering
  - stable finding ordering (severity → rule_id → file:line)
  - stable truncation/redaction rules
This is required for CI diffs and PR comments to be stable and non-noisy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:48:32.372701532-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T12:54:37.028914258-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T12:54:37.028914258-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented scan data model + stable report schema (src/scan.rs), deterministic finding ordering + fail policy, evaluator integration mapping to rule_id/severity, and unit tests; left extractors/CLI selection for follow-up beads.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:55:45.283494893-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","title":"Implement dcg scan CLI + file selection","description":"## Objective

Implement the `dcg scan` CLI surface area and file-selection logic.

This task is about:

- parsing args (`--staged`, `--paths`, `--git-diff`)
- selecting candidate files safely and predictably
- calling extractors and aggregating findings

It is *not* about the detailed extractors themselves (that is a separate task).

## Requirements

### CLI

- Add a `scan` subcommand with:
  - `--staged` (scan the git index)
  - `--paths <paths...>` (scan explicit paths)
  - `--git-diff <rev-range>` (scan files changed in a diff)
  - `--format pretty|json`
  - `--fail-on error|warning|none`
  - `--max-file-size`, `--max-findings`, `--exclude`, `--redact`

### File selection rules

- `--staged` should use `git diff --cached --name-only` (no content parsing via shell; prefer libgit2/gix later if we want).
- `--git-diff` should use `git diff --name-only <rev-range>`.
- Paths should be filtered by:
  - file size cap
  - ignore common vendor dirs (`target/`, `node_modules/`, etc.) unless explicitly included

### Output

- Pretty format groups findings by file.
- JSON format is a stable schema suitable for CI.

### Error handling

- Missing git repo / git failure should be a clear error for `--staged`/`--git-diff`.
- Non-existent paths should be a clear error.
- If extractor fails on a file, we should report an error finding but **not** crash.

## Acceptance criteria

- Running `dcg scan --paths scripts/e2e_test.sh --format json` produces valid JSON (even if no findings).
- `dcg scan --staged` runs in a git repo and produces deterministic file order.
- Unit tests exist for argument parsing and path filtering.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls (critical)
- `dcg scan` must never be naive grep.
- Only evaluate commands extracted from **executed contexts** (e.g., `run:` steps, Dockerfile `RUN`, Makefile recipes), not comments/docs.
- Every extractor must ship with negative fixtures to prevent “dangerous substring in data” findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:49:10.881718777-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T17:47:10.208059507-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T17:47:10.208059507-05:00","close_reason":"All children complete: CLI args (2.1), git integration (2.2), output formatting (2.3), and unit tests (2.4). dcg scan command is fully functional with JSON/pretty output, fail-on policy, and comprehensive test coverage.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:55:59.364769611-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:26.817595271-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.1","title":"scan: CLI args and command surface","description":"## Objective

Implement the `dcg scan` CLI argument parsing and subcommand UX.

This task is about **CLI surface area and flag semantics**, not the extractor implementations.

## Command selection modes (mutually exclusive)

- `dcg scan --staged`
- `dcg scan --paths <paths...>`
- `dcg scan --git-diff <rev-range>`

## Output / policy flags

- `--format pretty|json`
- `--fail-on error|warning|none`
  - `error`: non-zero exit if any *error/deny* finding exists
  - `warning`: non-zero exit if any warn/deny exists
  - `none`: always exit 0

## Safety / performance knobs

- `--max-file-size <bytes>` (default conservative, e.g. 1MB)
- `--max-findings <n>` (cap total findings)
- `--exclude <glob>` (repeatable)
- `--include <glob>` (repeatable; optional)
- `--redact <mode>` (e.g., `none|quoted|aggressive`) and/or `--truncate <n>`

## UX flags

- `--verbose` (include skipped-file reasons, extractor stats)
- `--top <n>` (limit printed exemplars in pretty output)

## Error handling

- Invalid flag combos produce actionable errors:
  - cannot combine `--staged` and `--paths`
  - cannot combine `--staged` and `--git-diff`
  - missing `rev-range` for `--git-diff`

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg scan --help` clearly explains modes and exit codes.
- Invalid flag combos produce helpful errors.
- Defaults are conservative (avoid scanning the world unintentionally).
","notes":"Implementation landed in commit 1d915d5: adds dcg scan CLI surface + handler skeleton (staged/paths/git-diff, format/fail-on/max limits/include/exclude/redaction/truncation placeholders) and unit tests for arg parsing + glob matching.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:03.443226256-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T13:20:23.839374506-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T13:20:23.839374506-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented scan CLI args and explain command","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:28.383412499-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","title":"scan: git integration (staged + diff file lists)","description":"## Objective

Implement file list discovery for:

- staged files (`git diff --cached --name-only`)
- diff ranges (`git diff --name-only <rev-range>`)

## Requirements

- Deterministic ordering.
- Clear errors when not in a git repo.
- Path filtering and excludes are applied consistently.

## Design notes

### Prefer NUL-separated output

To correctly handle filenames with spaces/newlines, prefer `-z` forms:

- staged: `git diff --cached --name-only -z`
- diff range: `git diff --name-only -z <rev-range>`

### Deleted / renamed files

Scanning should only attempt to read files that exist in the selected content source:

- For `--staged` scans, the *index* is the source of truth.
- For `--git-diff` scans, the working tree is usually sufficient (CI), but we must not crash on deleted paths.

Implementation should explicitly decide what to do with:

- deleted files (skip)
- renames (scan the new path)
- submodules (skip)

### Consistent filtering

All file lists must flow through one shared filter pipeline:

- include globs
- exclude globs
- max file size
- max files (optional)

## Test plan

Create a small fixture repo in unit tests and cover:

- staged add/modify
- staged rename
- staged delete (should not return deleted path)
- filenames containing spaces
- “not a git repo” (clear error)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan --staged` returns a deterministic, de-duplicated list of staged file paths.
- `dcg scan --git-diff <rev-range>` returns a deterministic, de-duplicated list of changed file paths.
- Deleted paths do not cause crashes or noisy errors; they are skipped with an optional debug log.
- Filenames with spaces/newlines are handled correctly (use `-z` plumbing).
- Filters (include/exclude/size) apply identically regardless of file list source.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive / noise controls
- Correct file selection reduces false positives by ensuring we only scan the intended diff/staged surface.
- Deleted paths and non-text files should be skipped deterministically (with optional debug log), not treated as scan failures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:08.538495954-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T13:31:02.109015085-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T13:31:02.109015085-05:00","close_reason":"Git integration complete: staged files, git-diff, NUL-separated parsing, space/newline handling, rename/delete handling, tests in place","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:33.475014249-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","title":"scan: output formatting + fail-on policy","description":"## Objective

Implement output formatting for scan results and the policy for failing (blocking) in CI/pre-commit.

This task defines the **stable JSON schema** and the **exit-code policy**.

## Output formats

### Pretty (human)

- Group findings by file.
- For each finding show:
  - location (file:line[:col])
  - extractor id
  - decision (ALLOW/WARN/DENY)
  - rule id (when matched)
  - short reason
  - truncated/redacted command preview
- Include a summary footer:
  - totals by decision/severity
  - total files scanned / skipped
  - elapsed time

### JSON (machine)

Schema requirements (v1):

- `schema_version: 1`
- `summary`:
  - `files_scanned`, `files_skipped`
  - `findings_total`
  - counts by `decision`
- `findings[]`:
  - `file`, `line`, optional `col`
  - `extractor_id`
  - `extracted_command` (truncated/redacted)
  - `decision`: allow|warn|deny
  - `severity`: info|warning|error
  - `rule_id` (when matched)
  - `reason` (when matched)
  - `suggestion?`

Determinism:

- Findings are sorted deterministically:
  1) `file` asc
  2) `line` asc
  3) `col` asc
  4) `rule_id` asc (or extractor_id if no rule)

## `--fail-on` policy

- `none`: always exit 0
- `warning`: exit non-zero if any `severity in {warning,error}` exists
- `error`: exit non-zero only if any `severity == error` exists

## Redaction / truncation

- Default: truncate long commands.
- `--redact` controls whether to redact quoted strings and other likely-secret spans.
- Redaction must not break JSON schema.

## Acceptance criteria

- Exit codes match documented policy.
- Outputs include rule id, severity, and reason when a match exists.
- Output ordering is stable for snapshot/golden tests.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Output should make “executed vs data” obvious to reviewers (include extractor kind + extracted command snippet).
- Redaction/truncation should avoid leaking secrets while still being debuggable.
- `--fail-on` should default to high-confidence catastrophic rules only; warn-first for the rest.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:13.636737749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T14:36:32.920265066-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T14:36:32.920265066-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented scan v1 JSON schema (schema_version + summary counts), deterministic finding ordering, pretty output grouped-by-file with extractor_id/decision/severity, and wired redact/truncate plumbing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:38.567223462-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:02.730946194-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:07.828229935-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:28.214491827-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:33.308987176-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:22:33.951854137-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:22:38.998889654-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","title":"scan: unit tests for CLI/path selection/output","description":"## Objective

Add unit/integration tests for `dcg scan` CLI parsing, file selection boundaries, and output stability.

## Coverage goals

### CLI parsing

- mutual exclusion of `--staged`/`--paths`/`--git-diff`
- defaults are conservative
- help text includes exit code semantics

### File selection

- respects `--exclude` / `--include`
- respects `--max-file-size`
- deterministic ordering

### Output correctness

- JSON schema v1 fields exist (schema_version, summary, findings[])
- deterministic ordering of findings
- `--fail-on` exit code mapping is correct
- redaction/truncation behavior is deterministic

## Logging requirements

On failure, tests print:

- the CLI invocation used
- captured stdout/stderr (truncated)
- parsed JSON diff vs expected (for json mode)

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests fail if we regress into extracting documentation strings.
- Snapshot/golden tests remain stable across runs.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive coverage
Ensure scan unit tests include explicit “must-not-find” cases:

- dangerous substrings in comments
- dangerous substrings in string literals / docs
- safe commands in executed contexts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:18.732314405-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T17:46:43.781639739-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T17:46:43.781639739-05:00","close_reason":"Added 28 new scan tests covering: JSON schema validation, summary calculations, redaction/truncation, fail-on policy, finding sorting, severity ranking, safe/dangerous command evaluation, and suggestion integration. Total test count: 468.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:43.658703242-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:12.924866928-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:18.01864202-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:23.11472507-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","title":"Implement MVP extractors (shell, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions, Makefile)","description":"## Objective

Implement the first set of conservative, high-signal extractors for `dcg scan`.

## Guiding principle

Only extract **executable contexts**. Avoid extracting from fields that are obviously documentation.

This task should explicitly encode these boundaries so false positives don't explode.

## MVP extractors

### 1) Shell scripts (`*.sh`)

- Extract candidate command lines from scripts.
- Keep it conservative:
  - ignore pure comments
  - ignore blank lines
  - treat each non-comment line as a candidate command line (we're not interpreting variables)
- Long term improvement (not required for v1): handle multiline `\` continuations.

### 2) Dockerfile

- Extract `RUN <shell form>` lines.
- Ignore JSON-array exec-form `RUN ["cmd", ...]` for v1 unless we add a tiny parser.
- Do NOT scan comments or `LABEL`/`ENV`/`ARG` values.

### 3) GitHub Actions workflow YAML

- Extract only `steps[].run` (string or block scalar).
- Explicitly ignore:
  - `name:` fields
  - `env:` values
  - `with:` values
  - docs/comments

### 4) Makefile

- Extract recipe lines (tab-indented).
- Handle `\` continuation for adjacent recipe lines.
- Ignore variable definitions and comments.

## Output requirements

Each extracted command must include:

- `file`, `line`, optional `col`
- `extractor_id`
- the extracted command string

## Tests

- Unit tests per extractor with minimal fixtures:
  - confirm *only* execution contexts are extracted
  - confirm correct line numbers
  - regression tests for common tricky cases (YAML block scalars, Makefile continuations)

## Acceptance criteria

- The extractors can be run on representative sample files without panicking.
- Unit tests demonstrate the extractor boundaries (no docs strings).
- Extracted commands are deterministic and stable for golden tests.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:49:26.918414721-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T20:49:26.918414721-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:04.621135514-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:31.911198902-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.1","title":"scan extractor: shell scripts (.sh)","description":"## Objective

Extract executable command contexts from shell scripts **conservatively**.

Shell scripts are noisy: many lines are assignments, control structures, or function definitions. A naive "every non-comment line" approach will create false positives.

## Rules (v1)

### 1) Ignore non-executable lines

Skip lines that are clearly not command invocations, e.g.:

- blank lines and pure comments
- variable assignments at start of line:
  - `FOO=bar`
  - `export FOO=bar`
- shell control keywords / structure:
  - `if`, `then`, `elif`, `fi`, `for`, `while`, `until`, `do`, `done`, `case`, `esac`, `{`, `}`
- function declarations:
  - `foo() {`
  - `function foo {`

### 2) Extract only likely command lines

For remaining lines:

- strip leading whitespace
- treat the line as a candidate command line
- do not attempt to evaluate variables

### 3) Line continuations (minimal support)

- If a line ends with `\`, join with the next line for extraction purposes (bounded join length).

### 4) Keyword prefilter (optional but recommended)

- If the line does not contain any enabled-pack keyword, skip extraction early.
  - This keeps extraction cheap and avoids scanning lots of irrelevant lines.

## Acceptance criteria

- Extracted commands include accurate line numbers (start line of a continued command).
- No panics on unusual Unicode or long lines.
- Does not extract obvious data-only assignments like `DOC="rm -rf"`.

## Tests

- Fixtures must include negative cases:
  - variable assignment containing dangerous substrings (must NOT extract)
  - comments mentioning dangerous substrings (must NOT extract)
  - control-structure lines (must NOT extract)
- Positive cases:
  - typical command lines (must extract)
  - continued command lines (must extract and join)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:16:54.946988626-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T18:06:12.741136236-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T18:06:12.741136236-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented shell and Dockerfile extractors with full test coverage","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:23.667222661-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.2","title":"scan extractor: Dockerfile RUN (shell form)","description":"## Objective

Extract executable shell commands from Dockerfile `RUN` instructions.

## Rules (v1)

- Extract `RUN <shell form>`.
- Ignore JSON exec-form `RUN ["cmd", ...]` in v1 unless we add a tiny parser.
- Ignore comments, labels, env, args.

## Acceptance criteria

- Correctly handles `RUN` with line continuations (`\\`).
- Provides correct line number for the start of the RUN command.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only Dockerfile `RUN` commands (shell form) and do not scan comments.
- Treat here-doc style `RUN <<EOF` carefully (avoid double-scanning; use a single extractor path).

### Tests
- Positive fixture: `RUN rm -rf /` (deny)
- Negative fixtures:
  - `# RUN rm -rf /` (comment)
  - `RUN echo "rm -rf"` (data-only mention)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:00.042137556-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T18:06:12.751011428-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T18:06:12.751011428-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented shell and Dockerfile extractors with full test coverage","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:28.75720403-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.3","title":"scan extractor: GitHub Actions steps[].run","description":"## Objective

Extract executable commands from GitHub Actions workflow YAML.

## Rules (v1)

- Extract only `steps[].run` values (string or block scalar).
- Ignore `name`, `env`, `with`, docs fields.

## Acceptance criteria

- Correctly handles block scalar indentation.
- Provides accurate line numbers.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Only extract from `steps[].run` (executed shell), not from:
  - `name:` fields
  - comments
  - YAML strings that are not executed

### Tests
- Positive fixture: workflow step `run: docker system prune -af` (deny when docker pack enabled)
- Negative fixtures:
  - `name: "rm -rf"`
  - comments containing dangerous strings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:05.13809578-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:15:21.268353308-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T19:15:21.268353308-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented conservative GitHub Actions workflow extractor: detect .github/workflows/*.yml|*.yaml, extract only steps[].run (single-line + block scalar) using shell-line extraction for comment stripping + keyword prefilter, with regression tests for line numbers and false-positive boundaries.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:33.852553708-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.4","title":"scan extractor: Makefile recipe lines","description":"## Objective

Extract executable recipe commands from Makefiles.

## Rules (v1)

- Extract tab-indented recipe lines.
- Handle `\\` continuations within a recipe.
- Ignore variable definitions and comments.

## Acceptance criteria

- Correctly identifies recipe boundaries.
- Provides accurate line numbers.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only recipe bodies (tab-indented command lines), not:
  - variable assignments
  - target names
  - comments
- Handle line continuations minimally and deterministically.

### Tests
- Positive fixture: a recipe line with `rm -rf /` (deny)
- Negative fixtures:
  - `VAR = rm -rf /` (assignment)
  - `echo "rm -rf"` (data-only)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:10.229383509-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:20:44.347941527-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T19:20:44.347941527-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Makefile recipe extractor (Makefile/makefile): extracts only tab-indented recipe bodies, includes backslash continuation lines, reuses shell extraction for comment stripping + keyword prefilter, and adds regression tests for boundaries + stable line numbers.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:38.948184716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","title":"scan extractors: fixtures + unit tests","description":"## Objective

Create fixtures and unit tests for all MVP extractors.

## Requirements

- Small fixture files per extractor (checked into tests).
- Tests assert:
  - only executable contexts are extracted
  - line numbers are correct
  - extraction is deterministic

## Critical false-positive guardrails

- Tests must explicitly include "dangerous substrings in data" cases for each extractor:
  - Shell: `FOO="rm -rf"` (must NOT extract)
  - GitHub Actions: `env: { X: "rm -rf" }` (must NOT extract)
  - Dockerfile: `ENV X="rm -rf"` (must NOT extract)
  - Makefile: `VAR = rm -rf` (must NOT extract)

## Acceptance criteria

- Extractor tests fail if we start extracting documentation strings or data-only fields.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive regression set
Ensure fixture suite includes explicit “do not flag” examples across file types:

- dangerous strings in comments
- dangerous strings in documentation fields (e.g., YAML `name:`)
- dangerous strings inside quoted arguments to non-executing tools

This is what prevents org-wide adoption from being derailed by noisy PR checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:15.323965301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:25:20.16151379-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T19:25:20.16151379-05:00","close_reason":"Added/extended extractor false-positive regression suite in src/scan.rs: Dockerfile ENV values ignored, GitHub Actions name/env/with ignored (only steps[].run extracted), plus existing shell assignment + makefile VAR assignment guardrails. Ran fmt/check/clippy/test.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:44.040251051-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:42.964281253-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:48.062306771-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:53.158858353-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:58.256989309-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","title":"Pre-commit integration for dcg scan (staged)","description":"## Objective

Make it easy for a team to adopt `dcg scan` locally by providing a first-class pre-commit integration path.

## Background

"You should run dcg scan" is not enough. Adoption happens when:

- installation is one command
- failures are actionable
- teams can choose warn-first rollout

## Proposed UX

### The core command

- `dcg scan --staged`

### Installation helpers

Pick one (or more) pragmatic options:

1. `dcg install-pre-commit` (writes `.git/hooks/pre-commit`)
2. `dcg print-pre-commit-config` (prints snippets for popular managers)

We should not assume any specific manager.

### Configuration

- optionally read `.dcg/hooks.toml` for:
  - scan globs
  - excludes
  - fail-on policy
  - max sizes

## Safety constraints

- Default policy should be conservative:
  - `fail_on = error` (high-confidence catastrophic rules)
  - `warning` findings do not block commits by default

## Tests

- Unit test that the generated `.git/hooks/pre-commit` script is correct (string snapshot is fine).
- E2E test (shell script) that runs `dcg scan --staged` on a small fixture repo and verifies expected behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can run one command and have pre-commit scanning enabled.
- The hook can be installed idempotently.
- Documentation exists in README or `dcg scan --help` output.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Pre-commit must be warn-first by default (fail only on catastrophic rules) to avoid punishing developers for FP noise.
- The hook should print actionable allowlist guidance (rule ID) so teams can converge on low-noise configs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:49:44.718141247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:28:39.604823404-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:09.84683307-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:37.005140608-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:42.102394302-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","title":"pre-commit: install/uninstall helper (git hooks)","description":"## Objective

Implement a helper command to install a pre-commit hook that runs `dcg scan --staged`.

## Requirements

- Idempotent install.
- Clear messaging about bypass (`--no-verify`).
- Support printing configs for hook managers (optional).

## Design notes

### Do not overwrite existing hooks silently

Pre-commit hooks are user-owned and often already exist.

Proposed behavior:

- If `.git/hooks/pre-commit` does not exist: create a minimal hook that runs `dcg scan --staged`.
- If it exists and was installed by `dcg` (detect via a sentinel comment): update in-place idempotently.
- If it exists and is *not* a `dcg` hook: do **not** overwrite; instead print explicit instructions for manual integration.

### Uninstall must be safe

- Only uninstall if the hook contains the `dcg` sentinel.
- Otherwise: refuse and explain (never delete/overwrite unknown user hooks).

### Exit codes / messaging

- The installed hook should:
  - surface scan output
  - fail the commit when `--fail-on` threshold is met
  - mention bypass path (`git commit --no-verify`) but encourage allowlisting / fixing

## Test plan

- E2E test in a temp git repo:
  - install when hook absent
  - install again (idempotent)
  - uninstall
  - attempt uninstall when non-dcg hook exists (should refuse)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan install-pre-commit` installs a working `.git/hooks/pre-commit` hook when none exists.
- Running install twice is a no-op (idempotent) and never duplicates hook lines.
- `dcg scan uninstall-pre-commit` removes only hooks it installed (sentinel-based) and refuses to touch other hooks.
- Hook messaging is clear about:
  - what failed
  - how to fix/allowlist
  - how to bypass (`--no-verify`) with explicit risk warning
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:55.636908818-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:53:58.511964208-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:19.453653841-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:10.086282153-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:15.185035321-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","title":"Pre-commit integration for dcg scan (staged)","description":"## Objective

Make it easy for a team to adopt `dcg scan` locally by providing a first-class pre-commit integration path.

## Background

"You should run dcg scan" is not enough. Adoption happens when:

- installation is one command
- failures are actionable
- teams can choose warn-first rollout

## Proposed UX

### The core command

- `dcg scan --staged`

### Installation helpers

Pick one (or more) pragmatic options:

1. `dcg install-pre-commit` (writes `.git/hooks/pre-commit`)
2. `dcg print-pre-commit-config` (prints snippets for popular managers)

We should not assume any specific manager.

### Configuration

- optionally read `.dcg/hooks.toml` for:
  - scan globs
  - excludes
  - fail-on policy
  - max sizes

## Safety constraints

- Default policy should be conservative:
  - `fail_on = error` (high-confidence catastrophic rules)
  - `warning` findings do not block commits by default

## Tests

- Unit test that the generated `.git/hooks/pre-commit` script is correct (string snapshot is fine).
- E2E test (shell script) that runs `dcg scan --staged` on a small fixture repo and verifies expected behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can run one command and have pre-commit scanning enabled.
- The hook can be installed idempotently.
- Documentation exists in README or `dcg scan --help` output.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Pre-commit must be warn-first by default (fail only on catastrophic rules) to avoid punishing developers for FP noise.
- The hook should print actionable allowlist guidance (rule ID) so teams can converge on low-noise configs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:49:44.718141247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:33:37.291756806-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:09.84683307-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:37.005140608-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:42.102394302-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","title":"pre-commit: install/uninstall helper (git hooks)","description":"## Objective

Implement a helper command to install a pre-commit hook that runs `dcg scan --staged`.

## Requirements

- Idempotent install.
- Clear messaging about bypass (`--no-verify`).
- Support printing configs for hook managers (optional).

## Design notes

### Do not overwrite existing hooks silently

Pre-commit hooks are user-owned and often already exist.

Proposed behavior:

- If `.git/hooks/pre-commit` does not exist: create a minimal hook that runs `dcg scan --staged`.
- If it exists and was installed by `dcg` (detect via a sentinel comment): update in-place idempotently.
- If it exists and is *not* a `dcg` hook: do **not** overwrite; instead print explicit instructions for manual integration.

### Uninstall must be safe

- Only uninstall if the hook contains the `dcg` sentinel.
- Otherwise: refuse and explain (never delete/overwrite unknown user hooks).

### Exit codes / messaging

- The installed hook should:
  - surface scan output
  - fail the commit when `--fail-on` threshold is met
  - mention bypass path (`git commit --no-verify`) but encourage allowlisting / fixing

## Test plan

- E2E test in a temp git repo:
  - install when hook absent
  - install again (idempotent)
  - uninstall
  - attempt uninstall when non-dcg hook exists (should refuse)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan install-pre-commit` installs a working `.git/hooks/pre-commit` hook when none exists.
- Running install twice is a no-op (idempotent) and never duplicates hook lines.
- `dcg scan uninstall-pre-commit` removes only hooks it installed (sentinel-based) and refuses to touch other hooks.
- Hook messaging is clear about:
  - what failed
  - how to fix/allowlist
  - how to bypass (`--no-verify`) with explicit risk warning
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:55.636908818-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:48:36.091372303-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T19:48:36.091372303-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented `dcg scan install-pre-commit` + `uninstall-pre-commit` (sentinel-based, idempotent, refuses unknown hooks), refactored scan CLI to support optional subcommands, and added temp-git-repo unit tests for install/uninstall + CLI parsing/conflict coverage.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:19.453653841-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:10.086282153-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:15.185035321-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","title":"pre-commit: .dcg/hooks.toml config parsing","description":"## Objective

Support a project config file that controls `dcg scan` behavior for pre-commit usage.

This exists so teams can adopt scanning without requiring everyone to remember long CLI flag sets.

## Proposed config file

- Path: `.dcg/hooks.toml`
- Scope: repository (committed, code-reviewed)

## Config goals

- Make rollout conservative and low-noise by default.
- Provide a single place to encode:
  - what to scan
  - what to exclude
  - how to redact
  - when to fail

## Suggested schema (v1)

```toml
[scan]
fail_on = "error"           # error|warning|none
format = "json"             # pretty|json (pre-commit usually uses pretty)
max_file_size = 1000000
max_findings = 50
redact = "quoted"           # none|quoted|aggressive
truncate = 200

[scan.paths]
include = ["scripts/**", ".github/workflows/**", "Dockerfile", "Makefile"]
exclude = ["target/**", "node_modules/**"]
```

## Validation behavior

- Unknown keys produce warnings (do not crash hook).
- Invalid values produce clear errors in `dcg scan` / `dcg doctor`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg scan --staged` (pre-commit integration) can load `.dcg/hooks.toml` automatically.
- CLI flags override config file values (explicit beats implicit).
- Config parsing failures are actionable.

## Tests

- Golden tests for config parsing:
  - valid config
  - unknown keys warnings
  - invalid enum values
- Integration test: config affects include/exclude and redact behavior deterministically.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","title":"pre-commit: E2E fixture repo + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add an end-to-end test that:

- creates a small fixture repo
- stages files with known findings
- runs `dcg scan --staged`
- asserts output summary and exit code

Logging must include per finding:

- file:line
- extracted command
- rule id
- reason

## Design notes

- The fixture repo should include at least:
  - a safe file (no findings)
  - a file with an executable destructive command in an executed context
  - a file with a *data-only* mention of a destructive string (should not be flagged)
- The test should assert both:
  - correctness (findings exist where expected)
  - ergonomics (output is actionable and stable)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test creates a temp git repo, stages files, runs `dcg scan --staged`, and asserts:
  - exit code matches `--fail-on` policy
  - output contains stable rule IDs and file:line locations
  - extracted commands are shown (redacted/truncated per policy)
- Test includes at least one negative fixture ensuring data-only mentions do not produce findings.
- Test output includes enough logging to debug failures without re-running locally.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","title":"pre-commit: docs + recommended rollout","description":"## Objective

Document how to adopt `dcg scan` in a pre-commit workflow **safely** (low-noise rollout that preserves developer/agent velocity).

## Required doc sections

### 1) What this is (and is not)

- `dcg` hook protects *interactive execution*.
- `dcg scan` protects repos by scanning *executable contexts* in files.
- Emphasize: extractor-based scanning (NOT naive grep) to avoid false positives.

### 2) Recommended rollout plan (warn-first)

- Start with:
  - narrow include set (workflows + Dockerfiles + Makefiles)
  - `--fail-on error` only
  - warnings informational for 1–2 weeks

- Expand gradually:
  - add `.sh` scripts
  - add additional extractors (compose, Terraform, GitLab CI, package.json)

### 3) How to interpret findings

- Meaning of `decision` and `severity`.
- How to read `rule_id` and why it is stable.

### 4) How to fix a finding

- Run `dcg explain "..."` for the extracted command.
- Prefer changing code to safer alternatives.
- If it is a false positive:
  - use `dcg allow` / `dcg allowlist add ...` (project or user)
  - explain allowlist safety constraints (expiration, risk_ack)

### 5) Privacy / secrets

- Recommend redaction defaults.
- Avoid printing full extracted commands in CI logs.

### 6) Example configurations

- `.dcg/hooks.toml` example for staged scanning.
- Example pre-commit hook snippet (and optional managers: husky/lefthook).

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can adopt scan-precommit without reading source.
- Docs explicitly warn against turning on warning-as-fail immediately.
- Docs show exactly how to resolve false positives safely.
